mechanics.mechanic: The deck itself: cards are physical craft-fragments with craft-discipline suits (song, joinery, glaze, thread, dough, dance); deckbuild is diegetic pack-packing, acquisition is teaching/trading/gifting, exhaust is pieces given away or performed to destruction.
Card quality as a waking scale: a card wakes if and only if it is played at or above an attention threshold; unattended spam-play produces inert, paling-prone cards. Things wake when watched.

The attention meter: attention is provided by watchers (woken audience-things, townsfolk, venue) and sustained by unbroken work-chains; it spends as a multiplier on the big play and gates waking. One visible currency sourced from the fiction.

The three resource-readings (node color, Standing, card wakefulness) never convert directly; every transfer between them must pass through a played card, making the act of play the world's only exchange gate.

Master economy invariant: attention spent always visibly arrives somewhere and unspent attention is never wasted, only returned (end-of-turn surplus seeps to the local table and returns in the next dawn's draw). A 'where it went' readout replaces discard-to-void.

Standing = gleam, the depletable fail-resource: depletes on failed/abandoned askings and unmoved audiences; at zero the engine goes quiet and the run ends into the Quiet Walk. Also gates soft benefits, so it reads as warmth, not a hit-point bar.

Standing-gain rule: attention played beyond what a card can hold (overkill delight above the waking threshold) converts to Standing at full rate, so pushing a performance past 'enough' is never waste and the biggest swings happen on stage.

Standing is a tagged resource, not a bare scalar: the kinds of delight that earned your gleam gate which askings lanterns lower and which shy materials state terms, giving builds identity ('bridge-grained' vs 'song-grained') with no class menu.

Per-turn attention income at turn-start ('the morning'): flat universal base plus a draw from the local table, where pale nodes grant richer dawns and end-of-turn surplus returns tomorrow — so traveling faded country carries a lawful reward baked into the physics.

Permanence doctrine and currency in one law: bench-woken cards stay woken for the run (the fired) while handsel money dims when idle and returns brighter when spent into delight (the held). Lawful anti-hoard pressure with zero bookkeeping.

Currency in wakefulness denominations (dull / warm / singing) instead of metals, with built-in tempo economy: idle money dims each round (gentle anti-hoard decay) while spending into delight returns change a shade brighter; held in a soft-capped purse (loops, not a number) whose upgrades are fired artifacts that slow the lapse.

Pricing as a read, not a rule: vendor costs expressed in wakefulness (dull/warm/singing), not coin counts; glad overpaying returns brightened change (a tempo-for-value choice, since gladness must be performed under attention). Anti-hoard handsel physics and the price system are one mechanic.

A crafting byproduct resource: big attended plays yield shaving tokens, convertible at rests and fairs into fresh handsels or minor pieces — a lawful bridge from the bench economy into the money and deck economies, with a season-scale lapse if unturned.

Resource acquisition as two visible gates, always in order: a gleam gate (which materials will state a condition this run, widening the market as Standing rises) then a performable micro-condition (played a song card, kept a chain going, hold an audience token).

The floor resource: zero-gate basic material for starter and comeback cards, always courtable at every node, keyed to the Quiet-Walk re-entry loop and the raw stock of handsel-whittling; its works carry the fastest paling-decay, making the upgrade path felt.

A tiered material market where each grade carries a distinct upkeep condition: singing-silver (unbroken play-chain, warm-kept or it reverts), laughing-riverwood (a 'the room is laughing' mood token, use-by season), festival-glass (venue-bound, acquirable only at high-attention nodes, grants passive attention).

Inventory freshness in place of weight limits — untended stock reverts to ordinary, making pack management a craft loop not a knapsack problem — plus introduction-jars as tradeable acquisition-skips that bank a courted material's introduction.

The escalating contract system: each asking is a physical token with legible demand-iconography; accepted askings ride the pack and warm as their season shortens; escalation is lawful and one-directional; the great askings are the boss tier (re-makings of world-scale systems).

The two-filter contract generator: asking TIER keys off peak-Standing-this-run (read from grain, never falls); asking OFFERS are gated by current Standing, so the failure spiral telegraphs in the lantern itself; next-tier silhouettes preview in the rafters.

The run's escalating pressure clock: map nodes visibly pale over time and needs compound; the Paling attacks board-state, resources, and routes but never touches the Standing meter directly, so players always know which losses are the world's weather.

The exact, deterministic compounding function for skipped nodes, chalked on the map itself: skip a three-ring town and you know precisely what five rings will ask and give back. Route strategy becomes almanac-reading — reading spirals is reading the future.

The lawful boss-contract generator: a great asking forms when a region's summed spiral-count crosses threshold, telegraphed seasons ahead (seams gather on the map), keyed to the brightest grain the country has heard of. The boss is always weather the player routed.

The contract-payoff bundle: reward scales directly off the gatepost spiral (rings in = load out), paid across every sub-economy at once — bright handsels, one taught card, one material introduction, map-intel chalked outward — so skipped-town compounding raises reward and difficulty in one number.

Standing resolution split in two: instant response at the current node, plus inbound rises drawn as source-stamped tellings walking the road-graph at fixed hearth-pace with readable arrival dusks; peak-Standing and lantern sizings update only on witnessed landings — nothing fires off-screen.

The pale-route harvest: a scout-plus-gather action whose yield scales with node paleness (richest where askings have compounded), never triggering on inhabited or answered nodes; last-red spent only as a catalyst on woken re-makings to slow that node's future paling.

Red thread as a place-marking consumable: delay one named knack's forgetting until your return (one thread per node, one season per thread), and the literal carrier of contract tokens riding the pack UI.

Standing-loss with a gift inside it: every point of gleam lost re-colors the current node as small ambient boons, claimable only by later visitors and future runs (never the spiller), always worth less than the gleam it cost — lawful compost that never invites a deliberate flop.

The contract UI and content-gating law: tier as hook-height, escalation as silhouette-preview, the failure spiral as offers descending the ladder; and by the same warm-dark law, ALL locked/out-of-tier content is hung as warm silhouettes in the rafters' dark — never greyed-out, padlocked, or cold black.

Standing has no HUD bar: the meter is the maker portrait's key light, dusk scenes are the check-your-Standing beat, grain-texture is the build-identity readout, quantized in showing-height and market-width bands with a lawful tally-chalk inspect overlay so it reads as steps.

The two persistences read at arm's length: a woken card carries the drawn line on the journey-piece's working edge (crackle-bloom = waking juice); handsels/shavings carry a breathing sheen animated by the economy's state (palm-brighten = payment juice); colorblind-safe by construction, line-versus-motion not hue.

The diegetic card-pool and class framework: each way is a card school; acquisition is walking-with-masters (map events) and season-shadowing (adopt a master's constraint for a segment, exit with an off-way card carrying their name); multi-way decks legal, grain bonuses reward focus. Archetype selection with no menu.

The tall-permanence archetype: the smallest, densest deck — few cards, heavily woken fired artifacts that persist all run and compound each turn, no spike turn; resource preference attention held in long unbroken chains; patient, low-variance, strongest boss-tier scaling, flavored toward the closed round.

The condition-puzzle control archetype: high-power shy-material cards each behind a legible micro-condition, with Standing as a pure gate (widens the market, never depleted by use); costs arrive only as outcomes (accepted terms that can go stale). Greedy value — dead cards in a dim hand, spectacular payoffs when the courtship lands.

The adversity-economy archetype: a route-risk specialist whose engine scales with node paleness — richest dawn income (fuller local tables), re-coloring as fuel, ripened compounded askings as harvest, boss-affine; steers the map toward the combing on purpose. Highest route risk in the game.

The sacrifice-and-velocity archetype: exhaust-heavy and self-thinning, always for conversion — inert/paling-prone pieces retired by last-lighting into local-table income (richer dawns downstream on this route) while a handsel engine keeps money bright by keeping it moving. Composts what other decks hoard.

The ratchet-tempo archetype: a thin, fast deck of cheap fine-grained one-shots that exhaust and never repeat (paper-bird pattern); many small contracts cleared quickly, with contract COUNT and PACE as the run's rising pressure in place of tier. (Carries the D-001 numeric obligation from I-022.)

The multiplier-spike archetype: an audience-engine deck of venue cards and transient crowd tokens (large, venue-bound, expiring when the fair moves on), attention throughput bent toward one enormous public turn where overkill converts to Standing at full rate. Highest variance in the game.

The run-frame: length fixed by a seasonal calendar track (spring to first still dawn), turn = one worked morning, leg = road between high days; the seasonal seep curve doubles as the escalation clock; two run-end triggers (Standing-zero, calendar end); first-gift at setting-out as diegetic loadout.

The run/map structure: a procedural circuit (a loop with a home note, not a branching tree toward a boss) of typed nodes on named edges through shed regions; route commitment in arcs at waymeets with lacing (spurs, figure-eights); route choice is the core strategic layer; the map screen doubles as the run's autobiography.

Edge-based (not node-based) relationship tracks: travel buffs living on specific road segments that deepen with tended re-travel — folded miles (reduced cost), shallow fords (weather resistance), return-leg gifts — the single home of all travel buffs, making the loop's second half rich.

The information layer: the approach screen IS the info screen (spiral count, asking-tier silhouettes, next-tier preview, dawn-richness, stall-warmth read off landscape art, no menus), and intel is a shared economy of found gifts from previous makers — your own past runs' marks persist, annotating the country.

Terrain as a legible two-dial system: elevation sets paling-rate (crowns fast, floors slow) and the dawn-ladder sets income geography (size from paleness, lateness from height); shed borders make 'where to sleep' a decision; crown-paths trade income for map-intel; light-dowsing scouts reveal cheapest re-color.

A rare anomaly region (once per several runs, off the main loop): pressure-free but income-free inside (boons stale, no dawn draw, no paling) with a known equinox release date whose one-dawn windfall equals the withheld seep exactly — a routing gamble about being there when it turns.

The moving hub node (draft/trade/teach/contest) rotating around the circuit on a visible advancing gradient; fair-intercept is a core routing subgame; the fair retells everything at twice the size (fastest Standing gain AND fastest ladder-raise in one act); the traveling lantern is the run's one persistent, most-personal contract source.

Micro-contracts on hamlet and roadside nodes: unsized, first-come, no reading taken (no lantern present), paying small immediate bundles of rest, welcome, and a handsel or two — a low-pressure income valve, sparse by law, absent on between-roads, priced below the apprentice-stuff comeback loop.

The finale-scheduling law: boss location computed from the player's own skip-arithmetic summed over inherited ring-states, telegraphed seasons ahead; a guaranteed year-end finale whose tier scales honestly to the year's weather (great asking, five-ring poem, or fair's-last-stage). Standing the crown gates the bright ending.

Rival-maker events: parallel-objective nodes where the player answers an asking alongside an NPC way working the same need; attention is the contested pool; winning takes the full reward and the telling, losing yields the smaller gift and costs tempo (never Standing directly — only the crowd's actual response moves gleam).

The boss-encounter structure: a telegraphed convergence where the player, as the chosen hands, accepts each way's gift as a legible boss-modifier (a raked ring raises the attention ceiling; fired staging protects board-state; courted introductions unlock proud materials; last-lightings become opening income). The boss as barn-raising, never a monster.

The fail run-end and meta-seed: Standing-zero triggers the Quiet Walk, a brief playable epilogue revisiting this run's woken works, one of which is chosen to persist into the meta-layer as a permanent audience-thing, unlock, or starting boon for the next maker. Not death — the world going shy.

The victory sequence as a playable epilogue: choose the wintering fire (porch/town/waymeet, each seeding a different meta-reward) then choose the bead that goes on walking (the run's one persistent gift); the year-string is the run-history UI, read bead by bead; new-game-plus framed as the next verse.

The meta-progression home: past runs' makers persist on the porches of the ways they walked with, appearing as audience figures who grant passive attention and grain bonuses to later runs; porches double as rest nodes where the hearth-right telling wakes a stretch of road around it.

The rest-node economy inverted from shop to gift: rest and healing are free (a right); the telling is a choice not a cost — the player picks one event from this run's history to tell at the fire and the house wakes around it, that event's tag becoming the night's boon. Selection, never expenditure.

The tutorial and discovery layer: laws are never stated in menus but demonstrated as folk-experiments the player performs (two pebbles, one winter), with a journeyman's notebook that fills in each law only after play has evidenced it; late-game the same notebook becomes the riddle-tracker for closed rounds.

Relic-class discovery: unmarked cards that fit no suit and never leave the pack, whose 'circulation' the notebook tracks as a measurable stat; the Bell of Hask as a persistent landmark fainter each save-generation; and the horizon-question — whether a sufficiently attended masterwork of the player's own might close a round — never answered, the collection screen quietly assembling evidence.

The text/meta layer: flavor as gift-provenance — every card quotes the person who gave it and names the giving, written at the moment of gift (the card taught at a gate arrives with that town's name), so the deck is an autobiography of teachers; at run-end the year-string review reads the beads in the same voice.
